geometric_interpretation_s2.description: When dΩ=0, search for local potential α s.t. Ω=dα. If ∫_M Ω≠0, potential is local only.
geometric_interpretation_s2.example: S²: Ω=J sinθ dθ∧dφ = d[J(1-cosθ)dφ], but ∫Ω=4πJ≠0 → not globally exact

singularity_topology.description: Singularities in potential α reveal non-trivial topology (poles in S² → H²(M)≠0)
singularity_topology.example: α singular at θ=0,π → de Rham cohomology H²_dR(S²)≅R

kahler_identity.description: In Kahler manifolds, Ω is simultaneously Kahler form ((1,1)-type), volume form, and Hopf bundle curvature

teaching_strategy_s2.description: Use S² as canonical example — simplest case (dim 2) exhibiting full richness of symplectic geometry
teaching_strategy_s2.example: Darboux + Kahler + topology + bundles all visible in S²

connections_to_existing: KahlerGeometry_S2 → extends contraprova to complex geometry
